mapping.a_element: Geometry of Interaction (GoI) as an algebraic weighting of paths in proofnets
mapping.b_element: Geometry of Interaction (GoI) applied to the category of sets and partial functions (GoI(Setp)) for path composition

mapping.a_element: Differential Interaction Nets extending MELL with non-deterministic behavior
mapping.b_element: Proof nets for multiplicative linear logic with units

mapping.a_element: Equational theory for GoI of MELL and its extensions
mapping.b_element: Direct and strongly normalizing cut elimination in proof nets

mapping.a_element: Reversible computation in Differential Interaction Nets
mapping.b_element: Path composition as the dynamics in GoI(Setp)

brief_justification: Both papers employ the Geometry of Interaction (GoI) framework to model dynamics in proofnets or related structures, with a focus on algebraic or categorical path-based semantics. The core machinery—GoI as a means of capturing computation via path composition or weighting—is shared, despite differences in the specific logical fragments (MELL with extensions vs. multiplicative linear logic with units).
